Well designed,clearly well intentioned,but lacking. The UI is excellent,the features offered are extensive and it functions very well. That being said it suffers from the following: 1 overwhelming and cringe worthy notifs (at least 6 about astrology back to back?) 2 low engagement. It is common to use the app just to see how many matches you get as opposed to meeting. 3 potentially flawed premise,many do not feel comfortable messaging first so their number of matches are effectively halfed.

My biggest gripe with Bumble is the subscription cost. $30 for one week,or $60 for one month. I had two conversations in the first 3 weeks,and then after subbing,I got 1 Like from using a spotlight. Only gives 1 spotlight per week,but tries to get you to use 2,so you have to buy another one... Dating apps have gone the way of the dollar bill. I've been using dating apps since 2012 with decent success,but they have all since changed the way they operate.
Took my money without permission and have to go through bank to sort it out. Wpuld gove 0 if I could
